howItWorks.intro: This sump pump size calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Total Head = Vertical Lift + (Horizontal Run x 0.1)
Required GPH = Basement Area x 1.5 x Water Risk Multiplier x Head Loss Factor
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What size sump pump do I need?
faq.answer: For most homes: 1/3 HP handles up to 1800 GPH, sufficient for average basements. 1/2 HP (3000 GPH) for larger basements or moderate water. 3/4-1 HP for high water table areas or long discharge runs.

faq.question: Do I need a backup sump pump?
faq.answer: Yes if your area floods frequently or you have valuable items in the basement. Battery backup pumps run 5-7 hours during power outages. A water alarm ($15-25) is the minimum every homeowner should have.
